blocks.body: To solve a system by graphing: (1) Graph both lines on the same coordinate plane. (2) Find the intersection point. (3) The coordinates of the intersection are the solution.

blocks.label: Example
blocks.body: Solve: y = x + 1 and y = -x + 5. Graph both. Line 1 has slope 1, y-int 1. Line 2 has slope -1, y-int 5. They intersect at (2, 3). Solution: (2, 3). Check: 3 = 2+1 ✓ and 3 = -2+5 ✓.
Parallel lines (same slope, different y-intercepts) never intersect — no solution. Same line (identical equations) — infinitely many solutions.

Graphing gives approximate solutions and is useful for visualizing the system. For exact answers, use substitution or elimination. Graphing is most reliable when the intersection is at integer coordinates.
